tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company needs a fast database to store customer orders and query them by different fields, like email, for quick lookups. Your job is to create a DynamoDB table, add an order item, use a Global Secondary Index to query by email, and validate the setup. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, and GSIs let you search data using keys other than the main one. Here’s how to do it with key steps and hints:
Create DynamoDB Table: Create a Item inside the table Create a Global secondary index Wait a few minutes for the table and GSI to be Active.
Create a Item inside the table
Wait a few minutes for the table and GSI to be Active.
Use Global Secondary Index to Fetch Data: (go to Explore Table items and click on the Scan or Query option for table that created.)
In the lab console, click Validate to check the DynamoDB table, item, and GSI query. Hint: Ensure the GSI query returns the correct item before validating.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
In the lab console, click End Lab from your dashboard and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the DynamoDB GSI challenge!
